namespaceId.title: namespace_id
namespaceId.description: The namespace id of the route.

originatorId.title: originator_id
originatorId.description: The originator id of the route.

statName.title: stat_name
statName.description: The name used for stats telemetry with the route.

description.title: description
description.description: A human-readable description of the route.

logoUrl.title: logo_url
logoUrl.description: A URL for a logo for the route.

from.title: from
from.description: The externally accessible URL for a proxied HTTP request.

to.title: to
to.description: The destination(s) of a proxied request.

redirect.title: redirect
redirect.description: Indicates that a route should be redirected.

httpsRedirect.title: https_redirect
httpsRedirect.description:
Indicates that the incoming scheme of a request will be swapped with
 "https".

schemeRedirect.title: scheme_redirect
schemeRedirect.description:
Indicates that the incoming scheme of a request will be swapped with
 the given value.

hostRedirect.title: host_redirect
hostRedirect.description:
Indicates that the incoming host of a request will be swapped with
 the given value.

portRedirect.title: port_redirect
portRedirect.description:
Indicates that the incoming port of a request will be swapped with
 the given value.

pathRedirect.title: path_redirect
pathRedirect.description:
Indicates that the incoming path portion of a request URL will be
 swapped with the given value.

prefixRewrite.title: prefix_rewrite
prefixRewrite.description:
Indicates that the incoming matched path prefix portion of a request
 URL will be swapped with the given value.

responseCode.title: response_code
responseCode.description: The response code used for the redirect. Defaults to 301.

stripQuery.title: strip_query
stripQuery.description:
Indicates that during redirection, the query portion of the URL will
 be removed. Defaults to false.

response.title: response
response.description: Indicates that a route should return a direct response.

status.title: status
status.description:
The status code that Pomerium returns for a static HTTP response.
 For example, 200.

body.title: body
body.description:
The response body that Pomerium returns for a static HTTP
 response.

loadBalancingWeights.title: load_balancing_weights
loadBalancingWeights.description:
Optional load balancing weights for upstream servers defined in to.
 If not specified, all upstream servers are assigned the same weight.
 If provided, len(to) must equal len(load_balancing_weights).

allowedUsers.title: allowed_users
allowedUsers.description:
Grants access to the route if the logged-in user's ID matches one of the
 given values. Deprecated in favor of the PPL user criterion.

allowedDomains.title: allowed_domains
allowedDomains.description:
Grants access to the route if the logged-in user's email address is for
 one of the given values. Deprecated in favor of the PPL domain criterion.

allowedIdpClaims.title: allowed_idp_claims

items.description:
Represents a JSON value.

 `Value` represents a dynamically typed value which can be either
 null, a number, a string, a boolean, a recursive struct value, or a
 list of values. A producer of value is expected to set one of these
 variants. Absence of any variant is an invalid state.
items.title: google.protobuf.Value

values.title: values
values.description: Repeated field of dynamically typed values.

allowedIdpClaims.description:
Grants access to the route if the logged-in user's session/user claims
 match one of the given values. Deprecated in favor of the PPL claim
 criterion.

prefix.title: prefix
prefix.description: Only match requests that have a path that begins with the given prefix.

path.title: path
path.description: Only match requests whose path is an exact match for the given path.

regex.title: regex
regex.description: Only match requests whose path matches the given regular expression.

prefixRewrite.title: prefix_rewrite
prefixRewrite.description:
Indicates that when a request is forwarded, the matched prefix (or path)
 will be swapped with this value.

regexRewritePattern.title: regex_rewrite_pattern
regexRewritePattern.description: A regular expression used to rewrite the path of a forwarded request.

regexRewriteSubstitution.title: regex_rewrite_substitution
regexRewriteSubstitution.description:
When a regular expression pattern is set, what the matched pattern will
 be replaced with.

regexPriorityOrder.title: regex_priority_order
regexPriorityOrder.description: Determines the ordering of routes that use regular expressions.

corsAllowPreflight.title: cors_allow_preflight
corsAllowPreflight.description:
Indicates that OPTIONS requests used for CORS can pass through
 unauthenticated.

allowPublicUnauthenticatedAccess.title: allow_public_unauthenticated_access
allowPublicUnauthenticatedAccess.description: Grants access to a route without requiring any authentication.

allowAnyAuthenticatedUser.title: allow_any_authenticated_user
allowAnyAuthenticatedUser.description: Grants access to a route for any logged-in user.

timeout.title: timeout
timeout.description: The per-route request timeout option. Cannot exceed global values.

idleTimeout.title: idle_timeout
idleTimeout.description:
The per-route request timeout for a connection that is idle with
 no data exchange.

allowWebsockets.title: allow_websockets
allowWebsockets.description: Allow websocket requests. Also disables the default route timeout.

tlsSkipVerify.title: tls_skip_verify
tlsSkipVerify.description:
Indicates that pomerium should perform no verification on upstream
 TLS connections. Any certificate for any hostname will be accepted.

tlsServerName.title: tls_server_name
tlsServerName.description:
The server name indication (SNI) for upstream TLS connections. If
 both tls_server_name and tls_upstream_server_name are set,
 tls_upstream_server_name will take precedence.

tlsUpstreamServerName.title: tls_upstream_server_name
tlsUpstreamServerName.description:
The server name indication (SNI) for upstream TLS connections. If
 both tls_server_name and tls_upstream_server_name are set,
 tls_upstream_server_name will take precedence.

tlsDownstreamServerName.title: tls_downstream_server_name
tlsDownstreamServerName.description: The server name indication (SNI) for downstream TLS connections.

tlsCustomCa.title: tls_custom_ca
tlsCustomCa.description:
The root certificate authorities (in PEM format) used to verify
 certificates of upstream TLS connections.

tlsCustomCaFile.title: tls_custom_ca_file
tlsCustomCaFile.description:
A file containing root certificate authorities (in PEM format)
 used to verify certificates of upstream TLS connections.

tlsCustomCaKeyPairId.title: tls_custom_ca_key_pair_id
tlsCustomCaKeyPairId.description:
A reference to a key pair containing root certificate authorities (in PEM
 format) used to verify certificates of upstream TLS connections.

tlsClientCert.title: tls_client_cert
tlsClientCert.description:
The client certificate to present to upstream TLS connections for
 mTLS.

tlsClientKey.title: tls_client_key
tlsClientKey.description:
The private key for the client certificate to present to upstream
 TLS connections for mTLS.

tlsClientCertFile.title: tls_client_cert_file
tlsClientCertFile.description:
A file containing the client certificate to present to upstream TLS
 connections for mTLS.

tlsClientKeyFile.title: tls_client_key_file
tlsClientKeyFile.description:
A file containing the private key for the client certificate to
 present to upstream TLS connections for mTLS.

tlsClientKeyPairId.title: tls_client_key_pair_id
tlsClientKeyPairId.description:
A reference to the key pair containing the client certificate
 and private key to present to upstream TLS connections for mTLS.

tlsDownstreamClientCa.title: tls_downstream_client_ca
tlsDownstreamClientCa.description:
The root certificate authorities (in PEM format) used to verify
 certificates for downstream TLS connections. If specified
 downstream clients will be required to provide a valid client TLS
 certificate.

tlsDownstreamClientCaFile.title: tls_downstream_client_ca_file
tlsDownstreamClientCaFile.description:
A file containing the root certificate authorities (in PEM format) used to
 verify certificates for downstream TLS connections. If specified
 downstream clients will be required to provide a valid client TLS
 certificate.

tlsDownstreamClientCaKeyPairId.title: tls_downstream_client_ca_key_pair_id
tlsDownstreamClientCaKeyPairId.description:
A reference to a key pair containing the root certificate authorities (in
 PEM format) used to verify certificates for downstream TLS connections. If
 specified downstream clients will be required to provide a valid client TLS
 certificate.

tlsUpstreamAllowRenegotiation.title: tls_upstream_allow_renegotiation
tlsUpstreamAllowRenegotiation.description:
Controls whether server-initiated TLS renegotiation is allowed for
 upstream servers.

setRequestHeaders.title: set_request_headers
setRequestHeaders.description:
Allows you to set both static and dynamic headers for upstream requests.
 Static values can be useful if you want to pass along additional
 information to upstream applications as headers, or to set a fixed
 authentication header on the request. Dynamic values enable you to pass
 ID and Access tokens from your identity provider to upstream
 applications. The following dynamic header values are supported:

 - ${pomerium.access_token}: OAuth access token from the identity provider.
 - ${pomerium.client_cert_fingerprint}: Short-form SHA-256 fingerprint of the presented client certificate.
 - ${pomerium.id_token}: OIDC ID token from the identity provider.
 - ${pomerium.jwt}: The Pomerium assertion JWT for the logged-in user.

removeRequestHeaders.title: remove_request_headers
removeRequestHeaders.description:
Headers to remove from upstream requests. This can be useful if you want to
 prevent privacy information from being passed to upstream applications.

setResponseHeaders.title: set_response_headers
setResponseHeaders.description:
Allows you to set static values for the given downstream response headers.
 These headers will take precedence over the global set_response_headers.

header.title: header
header.description: The name of the response header to rewrite (case-insensitive).

value.title: value
value.description:
The new value (or replacement substring, when `prefix` is set) to write
 back to the header.

prefix.title: prefix
prefix.description:
Only the portion of the header value that begins with this literal
 prefix is replaced; the remainder is kept.

items.title: RouteRewriteHeader
items.description:
A rewrite rule applied to a single response header before the response is
 returned to the downstream client.

rewriteResponseHeaders.title: rewrite_response_headers
rewriteResponseHeaders.description: Used to modify downstream response headers.

preserveHostHeader.title: preserve_host_header
preserveHostHeader.description:
Passes the Host (or :authority) header from the incoming request to the
 upstream service, instead of the Host header from the route's to URL.

passIdentityHeaders.title: pass_identity_headers
passIdentityHeaders.description:
Sends identity headers to all upstream applications. Identity headers
 include:

 - X-Pomerium-Jwt-Assertion: The pomerium assertion JWT for the logged-in user.
 - X-Pomerium-Claim-*: Each of the claim values specified by the jwt_claim_headers option.

 When not set explicitly, the global pass_identity_headers option will be used.

kubernetesServiceAccountToken.title: kubernetes_service_account_token
kubernetesServiceAccountToken.description:
A Kubernetes service account token used to authenticate to the
 Kubernetes API via Authorization: Bearer and Impersonate-User/Group
 headers.

kubernetesServiceAccountTokenFile.title: kubernetes_service_account_token_file
kubernetesServiceAccountTokenFile.description: A file containing a Kubernetes service account token.

kubernetesServiceAccountTokenKeyPairId.title: kubernetes_service_account_token_key_pair_id
kubernetesServiceAccountTokenKeyPairId.description: A key pair reference for a Kubernetes service account token.

enableGoogleCloudServerlessAuthenticatio.title: enable_google_cloud_serverless_authentication
enableGoogleCloudServerlessAuthenticatio.description:
Adds an Authorization: Bearer ID_TOKEN header for Google Cloud
 serverless upstream requests.

oneOf.title: IssuerFormat
oneOf.description:
Format used for the `iss` claim of JWTs Pomerium issues for upstream
 requests.

jwtIssuerFormat.title: jwt_issuer_format
jwtIssuerFormat.description:
Controls the format of the iss claim in JWTs passed to upstream
 services by this route.

jwtGroupsFilter.title: jwt_groups_filter
jwtGroupsFilter.description:
Allowlist of group names or IDs to include in the Pomerium JWT for
 this route.

jwtGroupsFilterInferFromPpl.title: jwt_groups_filter_infer_from_ppl
jwtGroupsFilterInferFromPpl.description:
Whether to add JWT groups from any PPL policies.
 Not supported in the open source version of Pomerium.

oneOf.title: BearerTokenFormat
oneOf.description: Controls how HTTP bearer token authentication is handled.

bearerTokenFormat.title: bearer_token_format
bearerTokenFormat.description:
Controls how authorization bearer tokens are interpreted for this
 route. When unset, the global bearer_token_format option is used.

dependsOn.title: depends_on
dependsOn.description:
Additional hosts that participate in chained login redirects for
 this route. Up to five hosts are supported.

id.title: id
id.description: Server-assigned unique ID of the policy.

namespaceId.title: namespace_id
namespaceId.description: ID of the namespace the policy belongs to.

originatorId.title: originator_id
originatorId.description: ID of the upstream object the policy originated from, when imported.

description.title: description
description.description: Free-form description of what the policy is for.

enforced.title: enforced
enforced.description:
An enforced policy is automatically applied to routes.
 Not supported in the open source version of Pomerium.

allowedUsers.title: allowed_users
allowedUsers.description: Grants access if the logged-in user's ID matches one of the given values.

allowedDomains.title: allowed_domains
allowedDomains.description:
Grants access if the logged-in user's email address domain matches one
 of the given values.

allowedIdpClaims.title: allowed_idp_claims

items.description:
Represents a JSON value.

 `Value` represents a dynamically typed value which can be either
 null, a number, a string, a boolean, a recursive struct value, or a
 list of values. A producer of value is expected to set one of these
 variants. Absence of any variant is an invalid state.
items.title: google.protobuf.Value

values.title: values
values.description: Repeated field of dynamically typed values.

allowedIdpClaims.description:
Grants access if the logged-in user's IdP claims include one of the
 listed key/value pairs.

rego.title: rego
rego.description:
Rego rules that authorize requests. All rules must allow for access to
 be granted.

sourcePpl.title: source_ppl
sourcePpl.description: The original PPL source the policy was compiled from, when available.

explanation.title: explanation
explanation.description: Free-form text shown to the user explaining why this policy applies.

remediation.title: remediation
remediation.description:
Free-form text shown to the user telling them how to gain access when
 this policy denies the request.

modifiedAt.title: modified_at
modifiedAt.description: When the policy was last modified.

oneOf.title: google.protobuf.Timestamp

modifiedAt.title: modified_at
modifiedAt.description: When the entity was last modified.

items.title: EntityInfo
items.description: EntityInfo is the basic metadata for an entity.

enforcedRoutes.title: enforced_routes
enforcedRoutes.description:
Routes that are automatically enforced by this policy.
 Not supported in the open source version of Pomerium.

oneOf.title: google.protobuf.Timestamp

modifiedAt.title: modified_at
modifiedAt.description: When the entity was last modified.

items.title: EntityInfo
items.description: EntityInfo is the basic metadata for an entity.

assignedRoutes.title: assigned_routes
assignedRoutes.description:
Info about routes with this policy assigned.
 Not supported in the open source version of Pomerium.

namespaceName.title: namespace_name
namespaceName.description: The name of the namespace for the policy.

policies.title: policies
policies.description: Structured sub-policies attached to this route.

raw.title: raw
raw.description: The serialized PPL document as raw bytes (typically YAML).

items.title: PPLPolicy
items.description: A policy expressed in Pomerium Policy Language (PPL).

pplPolicies.title: ppl_policies
pplPolicies.description: PPL policies attached to this route.

policyIds.title: policy_ids
policyIds.description: IDs of policies assigned to this route.

hostRewrite.title: host_rewrite
hostRewrite.description: Rewrites the Host or :authority header to a fixed value.

hostRewriteHeader.title: host_rewrite_header
hostRewriteHeader.description:
Rewrites the Host or :authority header using the value of another
 request header.

hostPathRegexRewritePattern.title: host_path_regex_rewrite_pattern
hostPathRegexRewritePattern.description:
A regular expression used to rewrite the Host or :authority header
 from the request path.

hostPathRegexRewriteSubstitution.title: host_path_regex_rewrite_substitution
hostPathRegexRewriteSubstitution.description:
When host_path_regex_rewrite_pattern is set, what the matched
 pattern will be replaced with to produce the Host header.

idpClientId.title: idp_client_id
idpClientId.description: Overrides the global identity provider client ID for this route.

idpClientSecret.title: idp_client_secret
idpClientSecret.description: Overrides the global identity provider client secret for this route.

values.title: values
values.description: The string values held by this list.

oneOf.title: StringList
oneOf.description:
Wrapper for a list of strings, used so map-valued fields can hold
 repeated string values.

idpAccessTokenAllowedAudiences.title: idp_access_token_allowed_audiences
idpAccessTokenAllowedAudiences.description:
Allowed audiences for validating IdP-issued access tokens for this
 route.

showErrorDetails.title: show_error_details
showErrorDetails.description:
If true, include policy explanation and remediation details in
 authorization error responses.

client.title: client
client.description: Provides an application with external tokens for MCP server access.

server.title: server
server.description: Indicates that this route fronts an MCP server.

clientId.title: client_id
clientId.description: The public identifier for an application.

clientSecret.title: client_secret
clientSecret.description: The secret known only to the application and the authorization server.

oneOf.title: OAuth2AuthStyle
oneOf.description:
How the OAuth2 client identifies itself to the authorization server when
 fetching tokens.

authStyle.title: auth_style
authStyle.description:
if unset, auto-detect which authentication
 style the provider wants by trying both ways and caching
 the successful way for the future.

scopes.title: scopes
scopes.description: Scope to control what data the OAuth2 server returns.

authorizationUrlParams.title: authorization_url_params
authorizationUrlParams.description: Additional parameters to add to authorization requests.

oneOf.title: UpstreamOAuth2
oneOf.description:
Configuration for OAuth2 authorization for an upstream service behind
 Pomerium.

upstreamOauth2.title: upstream_oauth2
upstreamOauth2.description:
Specifies that before the request reaches the MCP upstream server,
 it should acquire an OAuth2 token.

maxRequestBytes.title: max_request_bytes
maxRequestBytes.description:
The maximum request body size in bytes that can be sent to the MCP
 server (default: 4KiB).

path.title: path
path.description:
The path to append to the URL when returning the server URL in the
 .mcp/routes endpoint. Defaults to "/".

authorizationServerUrl.title: authorization_server_url
authorizationServerUrl.description:
The issuer URL of the upstream authorization server. Used as a fallback
 when PRM (RFC 9728) discovery fails, per the MCP spec's "abort or use
 pre-configured values" guidance.

mcp.title: mcp
mcp.description:
Model Context Protocol configuration for this route. Requires the
 runtime_flags.mcp feature flag.

maxConnections.title: max_connections
maxConnections.description:
The maximum number of connections that Envoy will make to the upstream
 cluster. If not specified, the default is 1024.

maxPendingRequests.title: max_pending_requests
maxPendingRequests.description:
The maximum number of pending requests that Envoy will allow to the
 upstream cluster. If not specified, the default is 1024. This limit is
 applied as a connection limit for non-HTTP traffic.

maxRequests.title: max_requests
maxRequests.description:
The maximum number of parallel requests that Envoy will make to the
 upstream cluster. If not specified, the default is 1024. This limit does
 not apply to non-HTTP traffic.

maxRetries.title: max_retries
maxRetries.description:
The maximum number of parallel retries that Envoy will allow to the
 upstream cluster. If not specified, the default is 3.

maxConnectionPools.title: max_connection_pools
maxConnectionPools.description:
The maximum number of connection pools per cluster that Envoy will
 concurrently support at once. If not specified, the default is unlimited.
 Set this for clusters which create a large number of connection pools.

circuitBreakerThresholds.title: circuit_breaker_thresholds
circuitBreakerThresholds.description: Circuit breaker thresholds for upstream requests on this route.

raw.title: raw
raw.description: The serialized PPL document as raw bytes (typically YAML).

oneOf.title: PPLPolicy
oneOf.description: A policy expressed in Pomerium Policy Language (PPL).

sshPolicy.title: ssh_policy
sshPolicy.description: PPL policy enforced before the SSH tunnel is opened to the upstream.

sshPolicyRego.title: ssh_policy_rego
sshPolicyRego.description: Rego rules evaluated before the SSH tunnel is opened to the upstream.

sshPolicyId.title: ssh_policy_id
sshPolicyId.description: ID of a shared policy whose rules also gate this SSH tunnel.

oneOf.title: UpstreamTunnel
oneOf.description:
Configuration for an upstream tunnel. Applies to routes whose upstream is
 reached through Pomerium's SSH proxy.

upstreamTunnel.title: upstream_tunnel
upstreamTunnel.description: Configuration for reverse tunneling to upstreams for this route.

consecutive5xx.title: consecutive_5xx
consecutive5xx.description:
The number of consecutive server-side error responses (for HTTP traffic,
 5xx responses; for TCP traffic, connection failures; for Redis, failure to
 respond PONG; etc.) before a consecutive 5xx ejection occurs. Defaults to 5.

interval.title: interval
interval.description:
The time interval between ejection analysis sweeps. This can result in
 both new ejections as well as hosts being returned to service. Defaults
 to 10000ms or 10s.

baseEjectionTime.title: base_ejection_time
baseEjectionTime.description:
The base time that a host is ejected for. The real time is equal to the
 base time multiplied by the number of times the host has been ejected and is
 capped by :ref:`max_ejection_time<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.max_ejection_time>`.
 Defaults to 30000ms or 30s.

maxEjectionPercent.title: max_ejection_percent
maxEjectionPercent.description:
The maximum % of an upstream cluster that can be ejected due to outlier detection. Defaults to 10% .
 Will eject at least one host regardless of the value if :ref:`always_eject_one_host<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.always_eject_one_host>` is enabled.

enforcingConsecutive5xx.title: enforcing_consecutive_5xx
enforcingConsecutive5xx.description:
The % chance that a host will be actually ejected when an outlier status
 is detected through consecutive 5xx. This setting can be used to disable
 ejection or to ramp it up slowly. Defaults to 100.

enforcingSuccessRate.title: enforcing_success_rate
enforcingSuccessRate.description:
The % chance that a host will be actually ejected when an outlier status
 is detected through success rate statistics. This setting can be used to
 disable ejection or to ramp it up slowly. Defaults to 100.

successRateMinimumHosts.title: success_rate_minimum_hosts
successRateMinimumHosts.description:
The number of hosts in a cluster that must have enough request volume to
 detect success rate outliers. If the number of hosts is less than this
 setting, outlier detection via success rate statistics is not performed
 for any host in the cluster. Defaults to 5.

successRateRequestVolume.title: success_rate_request_volume
successRateRequestVolume.description:
The minimum number of total requests that must be collected in one
 interval (as defined by the interval duration above) to include this host
 in success rate based outlier detection. If the volume is lower than this
 setting, outlier detection via success rate statistics is not performed
 for that host. Defaults to 100.

successRateStdevFactor.title: success_rate_stdev_factor
successRateStdevFactor.description:
This factor is used to determine the ejection threshold for success rate
 outlier ejection. The ejection threshold is the difference between the
 mean success rate, and the product of this factor and the standard
 deviation of the mean success rate: mean - (stdev *
 success_rate_stdev_factor). This factor is divided by a thousand to get a
 double. That is, if the desired factor is 1.9, the runtime value should
 be 1900. Defaults to 1900.

consecutiveGatewayFailure.title: consecutive_gateway_failure
consecutiveGatewayFailure.description:
The number of consecutive gateway failures (502, 503, 504 status codes)
 before a consecutive gateway failure ejection occurs. Defaults to 5.

enforcingConsecutiveGatewayFailure.title: enforcing_consecutive_gateway_failure
enforcingConsecutiveGatewayFailure.description:
The % chance that a host will be actually ejected when an outlier status
 is detected through consecutive gateway failures. This setting can be
 used to disable ejection or to ramp it up slowly. Defaults to 0.

splitExternalLocalOriginErrors.title: split_external_local_origin_errors
splitExternalLocalOriginErrors.description:
Determines whether to distinguish local origin failures from external errors. If set to true
 the following configuration parameters are taken into account:
 :ref:`consecutive_local_origin_failure<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.consecutive_local_origin_failure>`,
 :ref:`enforcing_consecutive_local_origin_failure<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.enforcing_consecutive_local_origin_failure>`
 and
 :ref:`enforcing_local_origin_success_rate<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.enforcing_local_origin_success_rate>`.
 Defaults to false.

consecutiveLocalOriginFailure.title: consecutive_local_origin_failure
consecutiveLocalOriginFailure.description:
The number of consecutive locally originated failures before ejection
 occurs. Defaults to 5. Parameter takes effect only when
 :ref:`split_external_local_origin_errors<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.split_external_local_origin_errors>`
 is set to true.

enforcingConsecutiveLocalOriginFailure.title: enforcing_consecutive_local_origin_failure
enforcingConsecutiveLocalOriginFailure.description:
The % chance that a host will be actually ejected when an outlier status
 is detected through consecutive locally originated failures. This setting can be
 used to disable ejection or to ramp it up slowly. Defaults to 100.
 Parameter takes effect only when
 :ref:`split_external_local_origin_errors<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.split_external_local_origin_errors>`
 is set to true.

enforcingLocalOriginSuccessRate.title: enforcing_local_origin_success_rate
enforcingLocalOriginSuccessRate.description:
The % chance that a host will be actually ejected when an outlier status
 is detected through success rate statistics for locally originated errors.
 This setting can be used to disable ejection or to ramp it up slowly. Defaults to 100.
 Parameter takes effect only when
 :ref:`split_external_local_origin_errors<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.split_external_local_origin_errors>`
 is set to true.

failurePercentageThreshold.title: failure_percentage_threshold
failurePercentageThreshold.description:
The failure percentage to use when determining failure percentage-based outlier detection. If
 the failure percentage of a given host is greater than or equal to this value, it will be
 ejected. Defaults to 85.

enforcingFailurePercentage.title: enforcing_failure_percentage
enforcingFailurePercentage.description:
The % chance that a host will be actually ejected when an outlier status is detected through
 failure percentage statistics. This setting can be used to disable ejection or to ramp it up
 slowly. Defaults to 0.

 [#next-major-version: setting this without setting failure_percentage_threshold should be
 invalid in v4.]

enforcingFailurePercentageLocalOrigin.title: enforcing_failure_percentage_local_origin
enforcingFailurePercentageLocalOrigin.description:
The % chance that a host will be actually ejected when an outlier status is detected through
 local-origin failure percentage statistics. This setting can be used to disable ejection or to
 ramp it up slowly. Defaults to 0.

failurePercentageMinimumHosts.title: failure_percentage_minimum_hosts
failurePercentageMinimumHosts.description:
The minimum number of hosts in a cluster in order to perform failure percentage-based ejection.
 If the total number of hosts in the cluster is less than this value, failure percentage-based
 ejection will not be performed. Defaults to 5.

failurePercentageRequestVolume.title: failure_percentage_request_volume
failurePercentageRequestVolume.description:
The minimum number of total requests that must be collected in one interval (as defined by the
 interval duration above) to perform failure percentage-based ejection for this host. If the
 volume is lower than this setting, failure percentage-based ejection will not be performed for
 this host. Defaults to 50.

maxEjectionTime.title: max_ejection_time
maxEjectionTime.description:
The maximum time that a host is ejected for. See :ref:`base_ejection_time<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.base_ejection_time>`
 for more information. If not specified, the default value (300000ms or 300s) or
 :ref:`base_ejection_time<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.base_ejection_time>` value is applied, whatever is larger.

maxEjectionTimeJitter.title: max_ejection_time_jitter
maxEjectionTimeJitter.description:
The maximum amount of jitter to add to the ejection time, in order to prevent
 a 'thundering herd' effect where all proxies try to reconnect to host at the same time.
 See :ref:`max_ejection_time_jitter<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.base_ejection_time>`
 Defaults to 0s.

successfulActiveHealthCheckUnejectHost.title: successful_active_health_check_uneject_host
successfulActiveHealthCheckUnejectHost.description:
If active health checking is enabled and a host is ejected by outlier detection, a successful active health check
 unejects the host by default and considers it as healthy. Unejection also clears all the outlier detection counters.
 To change this default behavior set this config to ``false`` where active health checking will not uneject the host.
 Defaults to true.

alwaysEjectOneHost.title: always_eject_one_host
alwaysEjectOneHost.description:
If enabled, at least one host is ejected regardless of the value of :ref:`max_ejection_percent<envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.OutlierDetection.max_ejection_percent>`.
 Defaults to false.

outlierDetection.title: outlier_detection
outlierDetection.description:
Passive health checking via outlier detection for upstream hosts on
 this route.

timeout.title: timeout
timeout.description:
The time to wait for a health check response. If the timeout is reached the
 health check attempt will be considered a failure.

initialJitter.title: initial_jitter
initialJitter.description:
An optional jitter amount in milliseconds. If specified, Envoy will start health
 checking after for a random time in ms between 0 and initial_jitter. This only
 applies to the first health check.

intervalJitter.title: interval_jitter
intervalJitter.description:
An optional jitter amount in milliseconds. If specified, during every
 interval Envoy will add interval_jitter to the wait time.

intervalJitterPercent.title: interval_jitter_percent
intervalJitterPercent.description:
An optional jitter amount as a percentage of interval_ms. If specified,
 during every interval Envoy will add ``interval_ms`` *
 ``interval_jitter_percent`` / 100 to the wait time.

 If interval_jitter_ms and interval_jitter_percent are both set, both of
 them will be used to increase the wait time.

unhealthyThreshold.title: unhealthy_threshold
unhealthyThreshold.description:
The number of unhealthy health checks required before a host is marked
 unhealthy. Note that for ``http`` health checking if a host responds with a code not in
 :ref:`expected_statuses <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.HealthCheck.HttpHealthCheck.expected_statuses>`
 or :ref:`retriable_statuses <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.HealthCheck.HttpHealthCheck.retriable_statuses>`,
 this threshold is ignored and the host is considered immediately unhealthy.

healthyThreshold.title: healthy_threshold
healthyThreshold.description:
The number of healthy health checks required before a host is marked
 healthy. Note that during startup, only a single successful health check is
 required to mark a host healthy.

reuseConnection.title: reuse_connection
reuseConnection.description: Reuse health check connection between health checks. Default is true.

noTrafficInterval.title: no_traffic_interval
noTrafficInterval.description:
The "no traffic interval" is a special health check interval that is used when a cluster has
 never had traffic routed to it. This lower interval allows cluster information to be kept up to
 date, without sending a potentially large amount of active health checking traffic for no
 reason. Once a cluster has been used for traffic routing, Envoy will shift back to using the
 standard health check interval that is defined. Note that this interval takes precedence over
 any other.

 The default value for "no traffic interval" is 60 seconds.

noTrafficHealthyInterval.title: no_traffic_healthy_interval
noTrafficHealthyInterval.description:
The "no traffic healthy interval" is a special health check interval that
 is used for hosts that are currently passing active health checking
 (including new hosts) when the cluster has received no traffic.

 This is useful for when we want to send frequent health checks with
 ``no_traffic_interval`` but then revert to lower frequency ``no_traffic_healthy_interval`` once
 a host in the cluster is marked as healthy.

 Once a cluster has been used for traffic routing, Envoy will shift back to using the
 standard health check interval that is defined.

 If no_traffic_healthy_interval is not set, it will default to the
 no traffic interval and send that interval regardless of health state.

unhealthyInterval.title: unhealthy_interval
unhealthyInterval.description:
The "unhealthy interval" is a health check interval that is used for hosts that are marked as
 unhealthy. As soon as the host is marked as healthy, Envoy will shift back to using the
 standard health check interval that is defined.

 The default value for "unhealthy interval" is the same as "interval".

unhealthyEdgeInterval.title: unhealthy_edge_interval
unhealthyEdgeInterval.description:
The "unhealthy edge interval" is a special health check interval that is used for the first
 health check right after a host is marked as unhealthy. For subsequent health checks
 Envoy will shift back to using either "unhealthy interval" if present or the standard health
 check interval that is defined.

 The default value for "unhealthy edge interval" is the same as "unhealthy interval".

healthyEdgeInterval.title: healthy_edge_interval
healthyEdgeInterval.description:
The "healthy edge interval" is a special health check interval that is used for the first
 health check right after a host is marked as healthy. For subsequent health checks
 Envoy will shift back to using the standard health check interval that is defined.

 The default value for "healthy edge interval" is the same as the default interval.

alwaysLogHealthCheckFailures.title: always_log_health_check_failures
alwaysLogHealthCheckFailures.description:
If set to true, health check failure events will always be logged. If set to false, only the
 initial health check failure event will be logged.
 The default value is false.

alwaysLogHealthCheckSuccess.title: always_log_health_check_success
alwaysLogHealthCheckSuccess.description:
If set to true, health check success events will always be logged. If set to false, only host addition event will be logged
 if it is the first successful health check, or if the healthy threshold is reached.
 The default value is false.

transportSocketMatchCriteria.title: transport_socket_match_criteria
transportSocketMatchCriteria.description:
Optional key/value pairs that will be used to match a transport socket from those specified in the cluster's
 :ref:`tranport socket matches <envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.Cluster.transport_socket_matches>`.
 For example, the following match criteria

 .. code-block:: yaml

  transport_socket_match_criteria:
    useMTLS: true

 Will match the following :ref:`cluster socket match <envoy_v3_api_msg_config.cluster.v3.Cluster.TransportSocketMatch>`

 .. code-block:: yaml

  transport_socket_matches:
  - name: "useMTLS"
    match:
      useMTLS: true
    transport_socket:
      name: envoy.transport_sockets.tls
      config: { ... } # tls socket configuration

 If this field is set, then for health checks it will supersede an entry of ``envoy.transport_socket`` in the
 :ref:`LbEndpoint.Metadata <envoy_v3_api_field_config.endpoint.v3.LbEndpoint.metadata>`.
 This allows using different transport socket capabilities for health checking versus proxying to the
 endpoint.

 If the key/values pairs specified do not match any
 :ref:`transport socket matches <envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.Cluster.transport_socket_matches>`,
 the cluster's :ref:`transport socket <envoy_v3_api_field_config.cluster.v3.Cluster.transport_socket>`
 will be used for health check socket configuration.

additionalProperties.description:
Represents a JSON value.

 `Value` represents a dynamically typed value which can be either
 null, a number, a string, a boolean, a recursive struct value, or a
 list of values. A producer of value is expected to set one of these
 variants. Absence of any variant is an invalid state.
additionalProperties.title: google.protobuf.Value

serviceName.title: service_name
serviceName.description:
An optional service name parameter which will be sent to gRPC service in
 `grpc.health.v1.HealthCheckRequest
 <https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/src/proto/grpc/health/v1/health.proto#L20>`_.
 message. See `gRPC health-checking overview
 <https://github.com/grpc/grpc/blob/master/doc/health-checking.md>`_ for
 more information.

authority.title: authority
authority.description:
The value of the :authority header in the gRPC health check request. If
 left empty (default value), the name of the cluster this health check is
 associated with will be used. The authority header can be customized for
 a specific endpoint by setting the :ref:`hostname
 <envoy_v3_api_field_config.endpoint.v3.Endpoint.HealthCheckConfig.hostname>`
 field.

host.title: host
host.description:
The value of the host header in the HTTP health check request. If
 left empty (default value), the name of the cluster this health check is associated
 with will be used. The host header can be customized for a specific endpoint by setting the
 :ref:`hostname <envoy_v3_api_field_config.endpoint.v3.Endpoint.HealthCheckConfig.hostname>` field.

path.title: path
path.description:
Specifies the HTTP path that will be requested during health checking. For example
 ``/healthcheck``.

send.title: send
send.description:
HTTP specific payload to be sent as the request body during health checking.
 If specified, the method should support a request body (POST, PUT, PATCH, etc.).

items.title: Payload
items.description: Describes the encoding of the payload bytes in the payload.

receive.title: receive
receive.description:
Specifies a list of HTTP expected responses to match in the first ``response_buffer_size`` bytes of the response body.
 If it is set, both the expected response check and status code determine the health check.
 When checking the response, “fuzzy” matching is performed such that each payload block must be found,
 and in the order specified, but not necessarily contiguous.

 .. note::

   It is recommended to set ``response_buffer_size`` based on the total Payload size for efficiency.
   The default buffer size is 1024 bytes when it is not set.

responseBufferSize.title: response_buffer_size
responseBufferSize.description:
Specifies the size of response buffer in bytes that is used to Payload match.
 The default value is 1024. Setting to 0 implies that the Payload will be matched against the entire response.

requestHeadersToRemove.title: request_headers_to_remove
requestHeadersToRemove.description:
Specifies a list of HTTP headers that should be removed from each request that is sent to the
 health checked cluster.

expectedStatuses.title: expected_statuses
expectedStatuses.description:
Specifies a list of HTTP response statuses considered healthy. If provided, replaces default
 200-only policy - 200 must be included explicitly as needed. Ranges follow half-open
 semantics of :ref:`Int64Range <envoy_v3_api_msg_type.v3.Int64Range>`. The start and end of each
 range are required. Only statuses in the range [100, 600) are allowed.

retriableStatuses.title: retriable_statuses
retriableStatuses.description:
Specifies a list of HTTP response statuses considered retriable. If provided, responses in this range
 will count towards the configured :ref:`unhealthy_threshold <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.HealthCheck.unhealthy_threshold>`,
 but will not result in the host being considered immediately unhealthy. Ranges follow half-open semantics of
 :ref:`Int64Range <envoy_v3_api_msg_type.v3.Int64Range>`. The start and end of each range are required.
 Only statuses in the range [100, 600) are allowed. The :ref:`expected_statuses <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.HealthCheck.HttpHealthCheck.expected_statuses>`
 field takes precedence for any range overlaps with this field i.e. if status code 200 is both retriable and expected, a 200 response will
 be considered a successful health check. By default all responses not in
 :ref:`expected_statuses <envoy_v3_api_field_config.core.v3.HealthCheck.HttpHealthCheck.expected_statuses>` will result in
 the host being considered immediately unhealthy i.e. if status code 200 is expected and there are no configured retriable statuses, any
 non-200 response will result in the host being marked unhealthy.

codecClientType.title: codec_client_type
codecClientType.description: Use specified application protocol for health checks.

send.title: Payload
send.description: Describes the encoding of the payload bytes in the payload.

items.title: Payload
items.description: Describes the encoding of the payload bytes in the payload.

receive.title: receive
receive.description:
When checking the response, “fuzzy” matching is performed such that each
 payload block must be found, and in the order specified, but not
 necessarily contiguous.

healthChecks.title: health_checks
healthChecks.description: Active health checks to run against upstream hosts for this route.

oneOf.title: LoadBalancingPolicy
oneOf.description:
LoadBalancingPolicy defines the strategy used to balance requests across
 upstream endpoints

loadBalancingPolicy.title: load_balancing_policy
loadBalancingPolicy.description:
The load balancing algorithm for upstream endpoints. Options:
 ROUND_ROBIN, RING_HASH, LEAST_REQUEST, RANDOM, MAGLEV.

healthyPanicThreshold.title: healthy_panic_threshold
healthyPanicThreshold.description:
If the percentage of healthy upstream hosts drops below this value,
 traffic will be sent to all hosts regardless of health status.

values.title: values
values.description: The string values held by this list.

oneOf.title: StringList
oneOf.description:
Wrapper for a list of strings, used so map-valued fields can hold
 repeated string values.

allowUpgrades.title: allow_upgrades
allowUpgrades.description: Allows HTTP upgrade requests to be forwarded upstream.

enabled.title: enabled
enabled.description: Toggle to enable/disable recording for this route.

oneOf.title: SessionRecording
oneOf.description: SessionRecording defines session recording settings for routes.

sessionRecording.title: session_recording
sessionRecording.description:
Configures session recording behaviour on this route.
 SSH specific.

createdAt.title: google.protobuf.Timestamp

modifiedAt.title: google.protobuf.Timestamp

oneOf.title: google.protobuf.Timestamp

modifiedAt.title: modified_at
modifiedAt.description: When the entity was last modified.

items.title: EntityInfo
items.description: EntityInfo is the basic metadata for an entity.

enforcedPolicies.title: enforced_policies
enforcedPolicies.description:
Policies that are automatically enforced.
 Not supported in the open source version of Pomerium.

oneOf.title: google.protobuf.Timestamp

modifiedAt.title: modified_at
modifiedAt.description: When the entity was last modified.

items.title: EntityInfo
items.description: EntityInfo is the basic metadata for an entity.

assignedPolicies.title: assigned_policies
assignedPolicies.description:
Info about policies assigned to this route.
 Not supported in the open source version of Pomerium.

namespaceName.title: namespace_name
namespaceName.description: The name of the namespace for the route.

oneOf.description:
`FieldMask` represents a set of symbolic field paths, for example:

     paths: "f.a"
     paths: "f.b.d"

 Here `f` represents a field in some root message, `a` and `b`
 fields in the message found in `f`, and `d` a field found in the
 message in `f.b`.

 Field masks are used to specify a subset of fields that should be
 returned by a get operation or modified by an update operation.
 Field masks also have a custom JSON encoding (see below).

 # Field Masks in Projections

 When used in the context of a projection, a response message or
 sub-message is filtered by the API to only contain those fields as
 specified in the mask. For example, if the mask in the previous
 example is applied to a response message as follows:

     f {
       a : 22
       b {
         d : 1
         x : 2
       }
       y : 13
     }
     z: 8

 The result will not contain specific values for fields x,y and z
 (their value will be set to the default, and omitted in proto text
 output):


     f {
       a : 22
       b {
         d : 1
       }
     }

 A repeated field is not allowed except at the last position of a
 paths string.

 If a FieldMask object is not present in a get operation, the
 operation applies to all fields (as if a FieldMask of all fields
 had been specified).

 Note that a field mask does not necessarily apply to the
 top-level response message. In case of a REST get operation, the
 field mask applies directly to the response, but in case of a REST
 list operation, the mask instead applies to each individual message
 in the returned resource list. In case of a REST custom method,
 other definitions may be used. Where the mask applies will be
 clearly documented together with its declaration in the API.  In
 any case, the effect on the returned resource/resources is required
 behavior for APIs.

 # Field Masks in Update Operations

 A field mask in update operations specifies which fields of the
 targeted resource are going to be updated. The API is required
 to only change the values of the fields as specified in the mask
 and leave the others untouched. If a resource is passed in to
 describe the updated values, the API ignores the values of all
 fields not covered by the mask.

 If a repeated field is specified for an update operation, new values will
 be appended to the existing repeated field in the target resource. Note that
 a repeated field is only allowed in the last position of a `paths` string.

 If a sub-message is specified in the last position of the field mask for an
 update operation, then new value will be merged into the existing sub-message
 in the target resource.

 For example, given the target message:

     f {
       b {
         d: 1
         x: 2
       }
       c: [1]
     }

 And an update message:

     f {
       b {
         d: 10
       }
       c: [2]
     }

 then if the field mask is:

  paths: ["f.b", "f.c"]

 then the result will be:

     f {
       b {
         d: 10
         x: 2
       }
       c: [1, 2]
     }

 An implementation may provide options to override this default behavior for
 repeated and message fields.

 Note that libraries which implement FieldMask resolution have various
 different behaviors in the face of empty masks or the special "*" mask.
 When implementing a service you should confirm these cases have the
 appropriate behavior in the underlying FieldMask library that you desire,
 and you may need to special case those cases in your application code if
 the underlying field mask library behavior differs from your intended
 service semantics.

 Update methods implementing https://google.aip.dev/134
 - MUST support the special value * meaning "full replace"
 - MUST treat an omitted field mask as "replace fields which are present".

 Other methods implementing https://google.aip.dev/157
 - SHOULD support the special value "*" to mean "get all".
 - MUST treat an omitted field mask to mean "get all", unless otherwise
 documented.

 ## Considerations for HTTP REST

 The HTTP kind of an update operation which uses a field mask must
 be set to PATCH instead of PUT in order to satisfy HTTP semantics
 (PUT must only be used for full updates).

 # JSON Encoding of Field Masks

 In JSON, a field mask is encoded as a single string where paths are
 separated by a comma. Fields name in each path are converted
 to/from lower-camel naming conventions.

 As an example, consider the following message declarations:

     message Profile {
       User user = 1;
       Photo photo = 2;
     }
     message User {
       string display_name = 1;
       string address = 2;
     }

 In proto a field mask for `Profile` may look as such:

     mask {
       paths: "user.display_name"
       paths: "photo"
     }

 In JSON, the same mask is represented as below:

     {
       mask: "user.displayName,photo"
     }

 # Field Masks and Oneof Fields

 Field masks treat fields in oneofs just as regular fields. Consider the
 following message:

     message SampleMessage {
       oneof test_oneof {
         string name = 4;
         SubMessage sub_message = 9;
       }
     }

 The field mask can be:

     mask {
       paths: "name"
     }

 Or:

     mask {
       paths: "sub_message"
     }

 Note that oneof type names ("test_oneof" in this case) cannot be used in
 paths.

 ## Field Mask Verification

 The implementation of any API method which has a FieldMask type field in the
 request should verify the included field paths, and return an
 `INVALID_ARGUMENT` error if any path is unmappable.
oneOf.title: google.protobuf.FieldMask

updateMask.title: update_mask
updateMask.description: Optional update_mask to only update a subset of the fields.
